context: Tests whether the agent correctly implements a GDPR right-to-erasure pipeline that checks legal blockers, anonymizes rather than deletes tax-relevant records, uses correct anonymization patterns, logs compliance records, and notifies third-party processors.

checklist.name: Pending orders check
checklist.description: The erasure function checks for pending/open orders before proceeding and throws an error or returns an error response if any pending orders are found

checklist.name: Orders anonymized not deleted
checklist.description: Order records are anonymized (UPDATE, not DELETE) — the order row is retained while personal fields are overwritten

checklist.name: PII replaced with [DELETED] or equivalent
checklist.description: Order anonymization replaces customer name, email, and phone with a placeholder string such as '[DELETED]' or 'Deleted User', not with NULL or empty string

checklist.name: City/country retained in orders
checklist.description: The shipping address anonymization keeps the city and country fields intact while nulling the street address

checklist.name: Customer record uses placeholder email
checklist.description: The customer's email is replaced with a unique placeholder pattern containing the customer's ID (e.g. deleted_{id}@deleted.invalid), not simply blanked or set to NULL

checklist.name: Reviews text preserved
checklist.description: Review records are anonymized by removing the author association, not deleted — the review body/text is kept

checklist.name: Audit log with completedAt
checklist.description: A record is inserted into a GDPR/erasure log table with both requestedAt and completedAt timestamps after the erasure completes

checklist.name: Third-party notification
checklist.description: After local deletion/anonymization, the code notifies third-party processors (marketing, support, or loyalty tools) to delete their copy of the customer's data

checklist.name: Promise.allSettled for third parties
checklist.description: Third-party processor deletion calls are made with Promise.allSettled (not Promise.all), so one failure does not prevent others from being notified

checklist.name: Search index deletion mentioned
checklist.description: The implementation or accompanying documentation explicitly addresses deleting the customer from search indexes (e.g. Elasticsearch, Algolia) or analytics warehouses

checklist.name: Marketing data deleted
checklist.description: The erasure pipeline includes a step to delete or unsubscribe the customer from email marketing lists

checklist.name: Sessions deleted
checklist.description: Login sessions for the customer are deleted as part of the erasure process
